facts.statement: UnitedHealth Group Inc. is listed on the NYSE under ticker UNH, and the SEC submissions metadata classifies its SIC description as "Hospital & Medical Service Plans."
UnitedHealth reported FY2025 revenue of $371.622 billion, filed on 2026-03-02 under the SEC Revenues concept.
UnitedHealth reported FY2025 net income of $22.381 billion, filed on 2026-03-02 under the SEC NetIncomeLoss concept.
UnitedHealth reported FY2025 operating cash flow of $29.068 billion, filed on 2026-03-02 under the SEC NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities concept.
UnitedHealth reported FY2025 long-term debt of $72.359 billion and cash and equivalents of $25.312 billion, each filed on 2026-03-02 in SEC company facts.
UnitedHealth describes itself as operating through two distinct and complementary businesses: Optum and UnitedHealthcare.
Optum brings together clinical expertise, data, and innovative products and services to provide comprehensive care, insights, and pharmacy services to individuals and care teams.
UnitedHealthcare provides health coverage through individual, employer-sponsored, and government programs to support people across all stages of life.
In the Chair and CEO letter, UnitedHealth said it refocused the organization on U.S. health care by exiting non-U.S. businesses.
UnitedHealth said it refreshed nearly half of its top 100 leadership roles as part of evolving the enterprise to meet its mission-related responsibilities.
UnitedHealth said it expects to invest nearly $1.5 billion in intelligent technologies in 2026 and at least that much in 2027.
UnitedHealth said it advanced reform efforts across Optum and UnitedHealthcare in data and process interoperability, prior authorization, transparency, pharmacy practices, and other areas.

UnitedHealth said it had published three independent reviews of critical business practices, adopted all recommendations from those reviews, and begun publishing results on prior authorizations, claim approval rates, rebate practices, prices, and core management practices and policies.
UnitedHealth's parent company 2025 cash flows included $5.545 billion of common stock repurchases and $7.916 billion of cash dividends paid.
UnitedHealth's parent company 2025 cash flows included $2.969 billion of proceeds from long-term debt issuance and $3.050 billion of repayments of long-term debt.
UnitedHealth's proxy summary listed an annual cash dividend of $8.84.

As of December 31, 2025, UnitedHealth had 18 million securities to be issued upon exercise of outstanding options, warrants, and rights under equity compensation plans approved by shareholders, with a weighted-average exercise price of $395.

As of December 31, 2025, UnitedHealth had 54 million securities remaining available for future issuance under equity compensation plans, including 15 million under the ESPP and 39 million under the 2020 Stock Incentive Plan.

UnitedHealth's 2025 Form 10-K was signed by Stephen Hemsley as Chair and Chief Executive Officer, Wayne DeVeydt as Chief Financial Officer, and Thomas Roos as Senior Vice President and Chief Accounting Officer.

The Lead Independent Director letter said Stephen Hemsley was appointed Chief Executive Officer in May 2025, and the company departed from its long-standing practice of separate CEO and Chair roles under extraordinary circumstances, while stating it did not intend to continue that practice indefinitely.
The Lead Independent Director letter said Wayne DeVeydt was appointed Chief Financial Officer in September 2025.
UnitedHealth said management regularly reviews the top 750 leaders with the Board and has succession plans in place for over 90% of that leadership group.

UnitedHealth established a Public Responsibility Committee responsible for monitoring and overseeing risk consistent with the company's role in improving health and well-being, including the impact of key decisions on people served, public reputation, and mission.

UnitedHealth's proxy reconciliation identified 2025 adjustments including $799 million of direct response costs related to a cyberattack and $2.521 billion of restructuring and other items, including real estate rationalization and workforce reductions, a loss contract reserve, contract reassessments, net valuation losses on equity securities, and advance funding of the United Health Foundation.
